class Page(AbstractPage, index.Indexed, ClusterableModel, metaclass=PageBase):
    title = models.CharField(
        verbose_name=_("title"),
        max_length=255,
        help_text=_("The page title as you'd like it to be seen by the public"),
    )
    title.required_on_save = True
    # to reflect title of a current draft in the admin UI
    draft_title = models.CharField(max_length=255, editable=False)
    slug = models.SlugField(
        verbose_name=_("slug"),
        allow_unicode=True,
        max_length=255,
        help_text=_(
            "The name of the page as it will appear in URLs e.g http://domain.com/blog/[my-slug]/"
        ),
    )
    content_type = models.ForeignKey(
        ContentType,
        verbose_name=_("content type"),
        related_name="pages",
        on_delete=models.SET(get_default_page_content_type),
    )
    content_type.wagtail_reference_index_ignore = True
    url_path = models.TextField(verbose_name=_("URL path"), blank=True, editable=False)
    owner = models.ForeignKey(
        settings.AUTH_USER_MODEL,
        verbose_name=_("owner"),
        null=True,
        blank=True,
        editable=True,
        on_delete=models.SET_NULL,
        related_name="owned_pages",
    )
    owner.wagtail_reference_index_ignore = True

    seo_title = models.CharField(
        verbose_name=_("title tag"),
        max_length=255,
        blank=True,
        help_text=_(
            "The name of the page displayed on search engine results as the clickable headline."
        ),
    )

    show_in_menus_default = False
    show_in_menus = models.BooleanField(
        verbose_name=_("show in menus"),
        default=False,
        help_text=_(
            "Whether a link to this page will appear in automatically generated menus"
        ),
    )
    search_description = models.TextField(
        verbose_name=_("meta description"),
        blank=True,
        help_text=_(
            "The descriptive text displayed underneath a headline in search engine results."
        ),
    )

    latest_revision_created_at = models.DateTimeField(
        verbose_name=_("latest revision created at"), null=True, editable=False
    )

    _revisions = GenericRelation(
        "wagtailcore.Revision",
        content_type_field="content_type",
        object_id_field="object_id",
        related_query_name="page",
        for_concrete_model=False,
    )

    # Override WorkflowMixin's GenericRelation to specify related_query_name
    # so we can do WorkflowState.objects.filter(page=...) queries.
    # There is no need to override the workflow_states property, as the default
    # implementation in WorkflowMixin already ensures that the queryset uses the
    # base Page content type.
    _workflow_states = GenericRelation(
        "wagtailcore.WorkflowState",
        content_type_field="base_content_type",
        object_id_field="object_id",
        related_query_name="page",
        for_concrete_model=False,
    )

    # When using a specific queryset, accessing the _workflow_states GenericRelation
    # will yield no results. This is because the _workflow_states GenericRelation
    # uses the base_content_type as the content_type_field, which is not the same
    # as the content type of the specific queryset. To work around this, we define
    # a second GenericRelation that uses the specific content_type to be used
    # when working with specific querysets.
    _specific_workflow_states = GenericRelation(
        "wagtailcore.WorkflowState",
        content_type_field="content_type",
        object_id_field="object_id",
        related_query_name="page",
        for_concrete_model=False,
    )

    # If non-null, this page is an alias of the linked page
    # This means the page is kept in sync with the live version
    # of the linked pages and is not editable by users.
    alias_of = models.ForeignKey(
        "self",
        on_delete=models.SET_NULL,
        null=True,
        blank=True,
        editable=False,
        related_name="aliases",
    )
    alias_of.wagtail_reference_index_ignore = True

    search_fields = [
        index.SearchField("title", boost=2),
        index.AutocompleteField("title"),
        index.FilterField("title"),
        index.FilterField("id"),
        index.FilterField("live"),
        index.FilterField("owner"),
        index.FilterField("content_type"),
        index.FilterField("path"),
        index.FilterField("depth"),
        index.FilterField("locked"),
        index.FilterField("show_in_menus"),
        index.FilterField("first_published_at"),
        index.FilterField("last_published_at"),
        index.FilterField("latest_revision_created_at"),
        index.FilterField("locale"),
        index.FilterField("translation_key"),
    ]

    # Do not allow plain Page instances to be created through the Wagtail admin
    is_creatable = False

    # Define the maximum number of instances this page type can have. Default to unlimited.
    max_count = None

    # Define the maximum number of instances this page can have under a specific parent. Default to unlimited.
    max_count_per_parent = None

    # Set the default order for child pages to be shown in the Page index listing
    admin_default_ordering = "-latest_revision_created_at"

    # An array of additional field names that will not be included when a Page is copied.
    exclude_fields_in_copy = []
    default_exclude_fields_in_copy = [
        "id",
        "depth",
        "numchild",
        "url_path",
        "path",
        "postgres_index_entries",
        "index_entries",
        "latest_revision",
        COMMENTS_RELATION_NAME,

            [

                ],
                _("For site menus"),
            ],
            {},
        ),
    ]
    settings_panels = [
        PanelPlaceholder("wagtail.admin.panels.PublishingPanel", [], {}),

    # Privacy options for page
    private_page_options = ["password", "groups", "login"]

class AbstractPage(
    WorkflowMixin,
    PreviewableMixin,
    DraftStateMixin,
    LockableMixin,
    RevisionMixin,
    TranslatableMixin,
    SpecificMixin,
    MP_Node,
):
    """
    Abstract superclass for Page. According to Django's inheritance rules, managers set on
    abstract models are inherited by subclasses, but managers set on concrete models that are extended
    via multi-table inheritance are not. We therefore need to attach PageManager to an abstract
    superclass to ensure that it is retained by subclasses of Page.
    """

    objects = PageManager()

    class Meta:
        abstract = True


# Make sure that this list is sorted by the codename (first item in the tuple)
# so that we can follow the same order when querying the Permission objects.
PAGE_PERMISSION_TYPES = [
    ("add_page", _("Add"), _("Add/edit pages you own")),
    ("bulk_delete_page", _("Bulk delete"), _("Delete pages with children")),
    ("change_page", _("Edit"), _("Edit any page")),
    ("lock_page", _("Lock"), _("Lock/unlock pages you've locked")),
    ("publish_page", _("Publish"), _("Publish any page")),
    ("unlock_page", _("Unlock"), _("Unlock any page")),
]

PAGE_PERMISSION_TYPE_CHOICES = [
    (identifier[:-5], long_label) for identifier, _, long_label in PAGE_PERMISSION_TYPES
]

PAGE_PERMISSION_CODENAMES = [identifier for identifier, *_ in PAGE_PERMISSION_TYPES]

# Wagtail Page-Model Contract Audit

## Step 1 — Orientation

**Project.** This is **Wagtail**, an open-source Django-based CMS (`README.md`, `setup.py` → package name `wagtail`). Content is modeled as a tree of *pages*; editors create/move/publish them in an admin, and they're served to visitors at their URL paths.

**Layout.** Everything ships under the single top-level package `wagtail/`. The pieces relevant to this audit:
- `wagtail/models/` — the core ORM layer, split into one module per concern (`pages.py`, `draft_state.py`, `locking.py`, `revisions.py`, `i18n.py`, `sites.py`, `workflows.py`, `reference_index.py`, `view_restrictions.py`, `audit_log.py`).
- `wagtail/query.py` — the `PageQuerySet`/`TreeQuerySet` (all tree + publishing query methods).
- `wagtail/actions/` — command objects for publish/unpublish/move/copy/delete/alias.
- `wagtail/admin/` — the editor UI: `views/pages/*`, `panels/`, `widgets/`, `ui/`, `viewsets/`, `api/`, `views/reports/*`.
- `wagtail/api/v2/` — the public REST API.
- `wagtail/contrib/` — optional apps (`redirects`, `forms`, `sitemaps`, `frontend_cache`, `settings`, `search_promotions`, `routable_page`, `simple_translation`).
- Serving glue: `wagtail/views.py`, `wagtail/url_routing.py`, `wagtail/sites/` + `models/sites.py`, `wagtail/templatetags/wagtailcore_tags.py`, `wagtail/rich_text/`.
- `wagtail/permission_policies/pages.py`, `wagtail/permissions.py`, `wagtail/signal_handlers.py`, `wagtail/management/commands/`.

**The page model itself:** `class Page(AbstractPage, index.Indexed, ClusterableModel)` at **`wagtail/models/pages.py:289`**. `AbstractPage` (`wagtail/models/pages.py:248`) is where the mixins are composed and `PageManager` is attached (`:265`).

---

## Step 2 — The Page-model contract

`Page` (`wagtail/models/pages.py:289`) is a `treebeard` MP-tree node assembled from seven mixins (`AbstractPage`, `pages.py:248-256`). A teammate splitting fields into satellite models must understand these layers, because dependents reach into all of them:

**Own fields** (`pages.py`): `title` (`:290`), `draft_title` (`:297`), `slug` (`:298`), `content_type` FK→ContentType (`:306`), `url_path` (`:313`), `owner` (`:314`), `seo_title` (`:325`), `show_in_menus` (`:335`), `search_description` (`:342`), `latest_revision_created_at` (`:350`), `alias_of` self-FK for aliases (`:392`). Generic relations `_revisions` (`:354`), `_workflow_states`/`_specific_workflow_states` (`:367`,`:381`). Search config `search_fields` (`:402`) — itself an enumeration of the contract (filters on `live`, `path`, `depth`, `locked`, `first_published_at`, `locale`…).

**Tree mechanics** (from `MP_Node`, imported `pages.py:39`): `path`, `depth`, `numchild` + `add_child`/`get_children`/`get_descendants`/`get_ancestors`/`get_parent`/`move`. The querying wrapper is `PageQuerySet` (`wagtail/query.py:314`) built on `TreeQuerySet` (`query.py:20`: `descendant_of`/`child_of`/`sibling_of`) and `SpecificQuerySetMixin` (`query.py:138`, `.specific()` at `:157`). Manager: `BasePageManager` (`pages.py:144`), `PageManager = BasePageManager.from_queryset(PageQuerySet)` (`pages.py:212`).

**Publishing state** (`DraftStateMixin`, `wagtail/models/draft_state.py:13`): `live` (`:14`), `has_unpublished_changes` (`:15`), `first_published_at` (`:19`), `last_published_at` (`:22`), `live_revision` (`:25`), `go_live_at` (`:35`), `expire_at` (`:38`), `expired` (`:41`).

**Revisions** (`RevisionMixin`, `wagtail/models/revisions.py:251`): `latest_revision` FK (`:254`); revisions stored generically via `content_type`/`base_content_type`/`object_id`.

**Locking** (`LockableMixin`, `wagtail/models/locking.py:11`): `locked` (`:12`), `locked_at` (`:15`), `locked_by` (`:18`).

**i18n** (`TranslatableMixin`, `wagtail/models/i18n.py:177`): `translation_key` (`:178`), `locale` FK (`:179`).

**Behavioral contract** — methods dependents call: `route_for_request` (`pages.py:499`), `route` (`:910`), `serve` (`:1271`), `get_url_parts`/`get_url`/`relative_url`/`full_url` (`:1351`,`:1447`,`:1489`), `specific`/`specific_deferred`/`specific_class` (via `SpecificMixin`), `save_revision`/`publish` (`:946`,`:1219`), `get_sitemap_urls`/`get_cached_paths` (`:1886`,`:1852`), `with_content_json` (`:1981`).

**How references are held** — three patterns a teammate must watch: (1) **hard FKs** to `Page` in satellite models; (2) **generic references** keyed on `base_content_type`+`object_id` (revisions, workflow states, reference index, log entries); (3) **transient queries** through `Page.objects`/`PageQuerySet`. Anything registered by content-type or by `get_page_models()`/`get_page_content_types()` (`wagtail/models/__init__.py:73-74`) is invisible to a plain grep for `Page`.

## Step 4 — Grouped by subsystem

**Serving / routing (core).** `views.serve` (`views.py:16`) → `Page.route_for_request` (`pages.py:499`) → `RouteResult` (`url_routing.py:1`); `Site.find_for_request`/`get_site_root_paths` (`sites.py:147`,`:218`) resolve the request to a root page via `root_page.url_path`. `serve_chain`, `authenticate_with_password` (`views.py:12`,`:35`). *Depends on:* tree (`path`/`url_path`), `live`, `route()`/`serve()`.

**Query layer (core).** `PageQuerySet` (`query.py:314`) + `TreeQuerySet` (`:20`) + `SpecificQuerySetMixin` (`:138`) + the specific iterables (`:655`,`:766`). *Depends on:* `path`/`depth`, `live`, `content_type`, `show_in_menus`, and the multi-table `specific` machinery.

**Templating / rich text (core).** `pageurl`/`fullpageurl`/`slugurl` (`wagtailcore_tags.py:18-52`), `PageLinkHandler` (`rich_text/pages.py:8`). *Depends on:* `get_url*`, `slug`, `Page.objects…specific()`.

**Publishing/workflow actions (actions/).** The eight action classes in `wagtail/actions/*` mutate `live`, `has_unpublished_changes`, `first_published_at`, `expire_at`, tree position, `alias_of`, revisions. Consumed by admin views and both APIs.

**Permissions (core).** `PagePermissionPolicy` (`permission_policies/pages.py:8`) + `PagePermissionTester` (`pages.py:2188`) compare `path` prefixes (`pages.py:2203`), read `locked_by` (`:2207`), `live` (`:2302`). `GroupPagePermission` (`pages.py:2147`) stores an FK to a page subtree.

**Locking (core).** `BaseLock`/`ScheduledForPublishLock` (`locks.py:11`,`:234`) branch on `isinstance(_, Page)` and read `locked`/`locked_by`/`go_live_at`.

**Admin editor UI (admin/).** The full `views/pages/*` CRUD set (create/edit/delete/move/copy/listing/search/ordering/history/usage/preview/revisions/lock/unpublish/workflow/convert_alias/choose_parent/bulk_actions), plus panels (`page_utils.py`, `publishing_panel.py`), UI columns/side-panels (`ui/tables/pages.py`, `ui/side_panels.py`), widgets (`AdminPageChooser`), viewsets (`viewsets/pages.py`), `site_summary.PagesSummaryItem`, `action_menu`, `mail.EmailNotificationMixin`, `models.get_object_usage`, reports (`aging_pages`, `locked_pages`, `page_types_usage`, `workflows`). *Depends on:* nearly the whole contract — `specific`, tree ops, `live`/`locked`/`latest_revision`, `content_type`.

**Admin API + Public API (admin/api, api/v2).** `PagesAdminAPIViewSet`, `PagesAPIViewSet` (`api/v2/views.py:437`) with `ChildOf/AncestorOf/DescendantOf` filters (`filters.py:161-217`) and serializer fields (`serializers.py:54-131`); API actions mirror the `actions/` classes. *Depends on:* tree queries, `live`, `url_path`, `content_type`, `locale`.

**Sites (core + sites/).** `Site` model holds `root_page` FK (`sites.py:108`); root-path caching depends on `root_page.url_path` + `locale` (`sites.py:239-261`).

**Reference/revision/workflow generic layer (models/).** `ReferenceIndex` (`reference_index.py:119`), `Revision`/`RevisionMixin` (`revisions.py:251`), `WorkflowState`/`WorkflowContentType` (`workflows.py:45`,`:99`), `PageLogEntry` (`pages.py:2629`) — all reference pages *generically* via `base_content_type`+`object_id`, registered by `get_page_models()`/content type.

**Contrib apps.** redirects (`Redirect.redirect_page`), forms (`FormSubmission.page`), search_promotions (`SearchPromotion.page`), settings (`page_url` helpers), sitemaps (`Sitemap.items` → `.live().public().specific()`), frontend_cache (purge-on-publish signal handlers), routable_page (`RoutablePageMixin`), simple_translation. Each either stores a page FK or hooks page signals / queries the tree.

**Comments & subscriptions (models/).** `Comment` (`pages.py:2669`), `CommentReply`, `PageSubscription` (`pages.py:2843`) — FK-attached editor collaboration data.

**Users & commands.** `PagePermissionsForm`/`BaseGroupPagePermissionFormSet` (`users/forms.py`); management commands `fixtree`, `set_url_paths`, `publish_scheduled`, `move_pages`, `create_log_entries_from_revisions`.

---

## Step 5 — Blast radius (ranked by risk)

**Tier 1 — visitor-facing serving; breaks silently for every anonymous visitor.**
1. `PageQuerySet`/`TreeQuerySet` (`query.py:314`,`:20`) — every listing, menu, and API query flows through `live()`/`descendant_of()`/`specific()`. Transient (no stored ref) but *universally* coupled; a moved field breaks all of them at once.
2. `views.serve` + `Page.route_for_request`/`route`/`serve` (`views.py:16`, `pages.py:499`,`:910`,`:1271`) — the request→page path. Drives every page view for visitors.
3. `Site` (`sites.py:90`, `root_page` FK `:108`) + `get_site_root_paths` (`:218`) — schema-level FK; if `url_path`/`locale` move, every URL resolution and root-path cache is wrong.
4. `contrib/sitemaps` (`sitemap_generator.py:9`) and `contrib/frontend_cache` (`utils.py:108`) — visitor-facing SEO + CDN correctness, driven by publish flow (`live`, `get_cached_paths`, publish signals).
5. Public API `PagesAPIViewSet` + tree filters (`api/v2/views.py:437`, `filters.py:161-243`) — headless consumers; breaks the same class of visitor traffic.

**Tier 2 — editor flows; break loudly in the admin.**
6. `EditView`/`CreateView`/`DeleteView`/`MoveConfirmView`/`CopyView` (`admin/views/pages/*`) — the core editor actions; read `live`/`has_unpublished_changes`/`specific`/tree. Driven by every editor session.
7. The eight `actions/*` classes + their admin/API wrappers — mutate publishing state and tree; a changed contract corrupts published state.
8. `PagePermissionPolicy`/`PagePermissionTester` + `GroupPagePermission` FK (`permission_policies/pages.py:8`, `pages.py:2188`,`:2147`) — path-prefix logic; a subtle break silently *grants or denies* access to whole subtrees.

**Tier 3 — schema-coupled satellites; hard FKs that break at the DB/migration layer.**
9. `Redirect.redirect_page` (`redirects/models.py:33`), `FormSubmission.page` (`forms/models.py:45`), `SearchPromotion.page` (`search_promotions/models.py:104`), `PageSubscription`/`Comment` (`pages.py:2849`,`:2675`), `PageViewRestriction`/`WorkflowPage`/`PageLogEntry` (`pages.py:2471`,`:2539`,`:2630`) — each holds a page reference in its own schema; safe against method changes but breaks if the FK target/PK semantics change.
10. Generic-reference layer: `ReferenceIndex`, `Revision`, `WorkflowState` (`reference_index.py:119`, `revisions.py:251`, `workflows.py:99`) — depend on `base_content_type`/`object_id` staying stable; highest "silent" risk because they resolve by content type, not by name.

**Tier 4 — lower-traffic / operational.** Reports (`aging_pages`, `locked_pages`), userbar, `site_summary`, management commands. Break visibly but affect fewer flows.

## Step 6 — Non-obvious dependents a text search misses

- **`ReferenceIndex` (`models/reference_index.py:119`)** — never says `Page`. It stores every page (and every field pointing at a page) under `base_content_type`+`object_id` (`:136-139`), registered via `get_page_models()`. A grep for `Page` in this file finds nothing meaningful, yet it indexes the entire model.
- **`WorkflowState` / `WorkflowContentType` (`models/workflows.py:99`,`:45`)** — reach pages through a `GenericForeignKey` on `base_content_type` (`workflows.py:121`), and `Page` even defines *two* generic relations (`_workflow_states`, `_specific_workflow_states`, `pages.py:367`,`:381`) precisely because the generic linkage is fragile. Easy to miss because there's no FK named `page`.
- **`Revision` / `RevisionMixin` (`models/revisions.py:251`)** — page content is serialized into revisions keyed generically; `PageRevisionQuerySet` filters on `base_content_type=get_default_page_content_type()` (`revisions.py:32`). Content-type-driven, not import-driven.
- **`contrib/frontend_cache/signal_handlers.py:17`** — does `apps.get_model("wagtailcore", "Page")` and `issubclass(model, Page)` at runtime, then connects `page_published`/`page_unpublished`. It depends on the page contract *entirely through the app registry and signals* — no top-level import to grep.
- **`api/v2/signal_handlers.py:13` and `admin/wagtail_hooks.py:134`** — wire behavior by iterating `get_page_models()` / registering `PageLogEntry` against `Page`. Config-time registration, invisible to a body-text search.
- **`coreutils.make_wagtail_template_fragment_key` (`coreutils.py:554`) and `get_dummy_request` (`:394`)** — utility helpers with generic names that quietly assume the page URL contract (`get_url`).
- **`contrib/settings/models.py:68` (`AbstractSetting.page_url`/`get_page_url`)** — a *settings* model that resolves arbitrary attributes to page URLs; lives far from the page subsystem and doesn't look page-related.
- **`blocks/field_block.py:967` (`PageChooserBlock`)** — reaches the model via a string `target_model` resolved through `resolve_model_string` (`:881`), i.e. by configuration, not import.
- **`locks.py:22` and `simple_translation/forms.py:53`,`:79`** — branch on `isinstance(obj, Page)` deep inside generic locking/translation code; the dependency is a runtime type check, not a query.
- **`admin/forms/models.py:74`** — builds a page FK form field generically (`to=Page`) inside the generic model-form machinery.
- **Management commands `set_url_paths.py:6` and `fixtree.py:11`** — operate directly on `url_path`/tree integrity; buried under `management/commands/` where nobody looks during a model refactor.

# Wagtail Page-Model Contract Audit

## Step 1 — Orientation

**Project.** This is **Wagtail**, an open-source Django-based CMS (`README.md`, `setup.py` → package name `wagtail`). Content is modeled as a tree of *pages*; editors create/move/publish them in an admin, and they're served to visitors at their URL paths.

**Layout.** Everything ships under the single top-level package `wagtail/`. The pieces relevant to this audit:
- `wagtail/models/` — the core ORM layer, split into one module per concern (`pages.py`, `draft_state.py`, `locking.py`, `revisions.py`, `i18n.py`, `sites.py`, `workflows.py`, `reference_index.py`, `view_restrictions.py`, `audit_log.py`).
- `wagtail/query.py` — the `PageQuerySet`/`TreeQuerySet` (all tree + publishing query methods).

## Step 2 — The Page-model contract

`Page` (`wagtail/models/pages.py:289`) is a `treebeard` MP-tree node assembled from seven mixins (`AbstractPage`, `pages.py:248-256`). A teammate splitting fields into satellite models must understand these layers, because dependents reach into all of them:

**Own fields** (`pages.py`): `title` (`:290`), `draft_title` (`:297`), `slug` (`:298`), `content_type` FK→ContentType (`:306`), `url_path` (`:313`), `owner` (`:314`), `seo_title` (`:325`), `show_in_menus` (`:335`), `search_description` (`:342`), `latest_revision_created_at` (`:350`), `alias_of` self-FK for aliases (`:392`). Generic relations `_revisions` (`:354`), `_workflow_states`/`_specific_workflow_states` (`:367`,`:381`). Search config `search_fields` (`:402`) — itself an enumeration of the contract (filters on `live`, `path`, `depth`, `locked`, `first_published_at`, `locale`…).

**Tree mechanics** (from `MP_Node`, imported `pages.py:39`): `path`, `depth`, `numchild` + `add_child`/`get_children`/`get_descendants`/`get_ancestors`/`get_parent`/`move`. The querying wrapper is `PageQuerySet` (`wagtail/query.py:314`) built on `TreeQuerySet` (`query.py:20`: `descendant_of`/`child_of`/`sibling_of`) and `SpecificQuerySetMixin` (`query.py:138`, `.specific()` at `:157`). Manager: `BasePageManager` (`pages.py:144`), `PageManager = BasePageManager.from_queryset(PageQuerySet)` (`pages.py:212`).

**Publishing state** (`DraftStateMixin`, `wagtail/models/draft_state.py:13`): `live` (`:14`), `has_unpublished_changes` (`:15`), `first_published_at` (`:19`), `last_published_at` (`:22`), `live_revision` (`:25`), `go_live_at` (`:35`), `expire_at` (`:38`), `expired` (`:41`).

**Revisions** (`RevisionMixin`, `wagtail/models/revisions.py:251`): `latest_revision` FK (`:254`); revisions stored generically via `content_type`/`base_content_type`/`object_id`.

**Locking** (`LockableMixin`, `wagtail/models/locking.py:11`): `locked` (`:12`), `locked_at` (`:15`), `locked_by` (`:18`).

**i18n** (`TranslatableMixin`, `wagtail/models/i18n.py:177`): `translation_key` (`:178`), `locale` FK (`:179`).

**Behavioral contract** — methods dependents call: `route_for_request` (`pages.py:499`), `route` (`:910`), `serve` (`:1271`), `get_url_parts`/`get_url`/`relative_url`/`full_url` (`:1351`,`:1447`,`:1489`), `specific`/`specific_deferred`/`specific_class` (via `SpecificMixin`), `save_revision`/`publish` (`:946`,`:1219`), `get_sitemap_urls`/`get_cached_paths` (`:1886`,`:1852`), `with_content_json` (`:1981`).

**How references are held** — three patterns a teammate must watch: (1) **hard FKs** to `Page` in satellite models; (2) **generic references** keyed on `base_content_type`+`object_id` (revisions, workflow states, reference index, log entries); (3) **transient queries** through `Page.objects`/`PageQuerySet`. Anything registered by content-type or by `get_page_models()`/`get_page_content_types()` (`wagtail/models/__init__.py:73-74`) is invisible to a plain grep for `Page`.

## Step 4 — Grouped by subsystem

**Serving / routing (core).** `views.serve` (`views.py:16`) → `Page.route_for_request` (`pages.py:499`) → `RouteResult` (`url_routing.py:1`); `Site.find_for_request`/`get_site_root_paths` (`sites.py:147`,`:218`) resolve the request to a root page via `root_page.url_path`. `serve_chain`, `authenticate_with_password` (`views.py:12`,`:35`). *Depends on:* tree (`path`/`url_path`), `live`, `route()`/`serve()`.

**Query layer (core).** `PageQuerySet` (`query.py:314`) + `TreeQuerySet` (`:20`) + `SpecificQuerySetMixin` (`:138`) + the specific iterables (`:655`,`:766`). *Depends on:* `path`/`depth`, `live`, `content_type`, `show_in_menus`, and the multi-table `specific` machinery.

**Templating / rich text (core).** `pageurl`/`fullpageurl`/`slugurl` (`wagtailcore_tags.py:18-52`), `PageLinkHandler` (`rich_text/pages.py:8`). *Depends on:* `get_url*`, `slug`, `Page.objects…specific()`.

**Publishing/workflow actions (actions/).** The eight action classes in `wagtail/actions/*` mutate `live`, `has_unpublished_changes`, `first_published_at`, `expire_at`, tree position, `alias_of`, revisions. Consumed by admin views and both APIs.

**Permissions (core).** `PagePermissionPolicy` (`permission_policies/pages.py:8`) + `PagePermissionTester` (`pages.py:2188`) compare `path` prefixes (`pages.py:2203`), read `locked_by` (`:2207`), `live` (`:2302`). `GroupPagePermission` (`pages.py:2147`) stores an FK to a page subtree.

**Locking (core).** `BaseLock`/`ScheduledForPublishLock` (`locks.py:11`,`:234`) branch on `isinstance(_, Page)` and read `locked`/`locked_by`/`go_live_at`.

**Admin editor UI (admin/).** The full `views/pages/*` CRUD set (create/edit/delete/move/copy/listing/search/ordering/history/usage/preview/revisions/lock/unpublish/workflow/convert_alias/choose_parent/bulk_actions), plus panels (`page_utils.py`, `publishing_panel.py`), UI columns/side-panels (`ui/tables/pages.py`, `ui/side_panels.py`), widgets (`AdminPageChooser`), viewsets (`viewsets/pages.py`), `site_summary.PagesSummaryItem`, `action_menu`, `mail.EmailNotificationMixin`, `models.get_object_usage`, reports (`aging_pages`, `locked_pages`, `page_types_usage`, `workflows`). *Depends on:* nearly the whole contract — `specific`, tree ops, `live`/`locked`/`latest_revision`, `content_type`.

**Admin API + Public API (admin/api, api/v2).** `PagesAdminAPIViewSet`, `PagesAPIViewSet` (`api/v2/views.py:437`) with `ChildOf/AncestorOf/DescendantOf` filters (`filters.py:161-217`) and serializer fields (`serializers.py:54-131`); API actions mirror the `actions/` classes. *Depends on:* tree queries, `live`, `url_path`, `content_type`, `locale`.

**Sites (core + sites/).** `Site` model holds `root_page` FK (`sites.py:108`); root-path caching depends on `root_page.url_path` + `locale` (`sites.py:239-261`).

**Reference/revision/workflow generic layer (models/).** `ReferenceIndex` (`reference_index.py:119`), `Revision`/`RevisionMixin` (`revisions.py:251`), `WorkflowState`/`WorkflowContentType` (`workflows.py:45`,`:99`), `PageLogEntry` (`pages.py:2629`) — all reference pages *generically* via `base_content_type`+`object_id`, registered by `get_page_models()`/content type.

**Contrib apps.** redirects (`Redirect.redirect_page`), forms (`FormSubmission.page`), search_promotions (`SearchPromotion.page`), settings (`page_url` helpers), sitemaps (`Sitemap.items` → `.live().public().specific()`), frontend_cache (purge-on-publish signal handlers), routable_page (`RoutablePageMixin`), simple_translation. Each either stores a page FK or hooks page signals / queries the tree.

**Comments & subscriptions (models/).** `Comment` (`pages.py:2669`), `CommentReply`, `PageSubscription` (`pages.py:2843`) — FK-attached editor collaboration data.

**Users & commands.** `PagePermissionsForm`/`BaseGroupPagePermissionFormSet` (`users/forms.py`); management commands `fixtree`, `set_url_paths`, `publish_scheduled`, `move_pages`, `create_log_entries_from_revisions`.

## Step 5 — Blast radius (ranked by risk)

**Tier 1 — visitor-facing serving; breaks silently for every anonymous visitor.**
1. `PageQuerySet`/`TreeQuerySet` (`query.py:314`,`:20`) — every listing, menu, and API query flows through `live()`/`descendant_of()`/`specific()`. Transient (no stored ref) but *universally* coupled; a moved field breaks all of them at once.
2. `views.serve` + `Page.route_for_request`/`route`/`serve` (`views.py:16`, `pages.py:499`,`:910`,`:1271`) — the request→page path. Drives every page view for visitors.
3. `Site` (`sites.py:90`, `root_page` FK `:108`) + `get_site_root_paths` (`:218`) — schema-level FK; if `url_path`/`locale` move, every URL resolution and root-path cache is wrong.
4. `contrib/sitemaps` (`sitemap_generator.py:9`) and `contrib/frontend_cache` (`utils.py:108`) — visitor-facing SEO + CDN correctness, driven by publish flow (`live`, `get_cached_paths`, publish signals).
5. Public API `PagesAPIViewSet` + tree filters (`api/v2/views.py:437`, `filters.py:161-243`) — headless consumers; breaks the same class of visitor traffic.

**Tier 2 — editor flows; break loudly in the admin.**
6. `EditView`/`CreateView`/`DeleteView`/`MoveConfirmView`/`CopyView` (`admin/views/pages/*`) — the core editor actions; read `live`/`has_unpublished_changes`/`specific`/tree. Driven by every editor session.
7. The eight `actions/*` classes + their admin/API wrappers — mutate publishing state and tree; a changed contract corrupts published state.
8. `PagePermissionPolicy`/`PagePermissionTester` + `GroupPagePermission` FK (`permission_policies/pages.py:8`, `pages.py:2188`,`:2147`) — path-prefix logic; a subtle break silently *grants or denies* access to whole subtrees.

**Tier 3 — schema-coupled satellites; hard FKs that break at the DB/migration layer.**
9. `Redirect.redirect_page` (`redirects/models.py:33`), `FormSubmission.page` (`forms/models.py:45`), `SearchPromotion.page` (`search_promotions/models.py:104`), `PageSubscription`/`Comment` (`pages.py:2849`,`:2675`), `PageViewRestriction`/`WorkflowPage`/`PageLogEntry` (`pages.py:2471`,`:2539`,`:2630`) — each holds a page reference in its own schema; safe against method changes but breaks if the FK target/PK semantics change.
10. Generic-reference layer: `ReferenceIndex`, `Revision`, `WorkflowState` (`reference_index.py:119`, `revisions.py:251`, `workflows.py:99`) — depend on `base_content_type`/`object_id` staying stable; highest "silent" risk because they resolve by content type, not by name.

**Tier 4 — lower-traffic / operational.** Reports (`aging_pages`, `locked_pages`), userbar, `site_summary`, management commands. Break visibly but affect fewer flows.

## Step 6 — Non-obvious dependents a text search misses

- **`ReferenceIndex` (`models/reference_index.py:119`)** — never says `Page`. It stores every page (and every field pointing at a page) under `base_content_type`+`object_id` (`:136-139`), registered via `get_page_models()`. A grep for `Page` in this file finds nothing meaningful, yet it indexes the entire model.
- **`WorkflowState` / `WorkflowContentType` (`models/workflows.py:99`,`:45`)** — reach pages through a `GenericForeignKey` on `base_content_type` (`workflows.py:121`), and `Page` even defines *two* generic relations (`_workflow_states`, `_specific_workflow_states`, `pages.py:367`,`:381`) precisely because the generic linkage is fragile. Easy to miss because there's no FK named `page`.
- **`Revision` / `RevisionMixin` (`models/revisions.py:251`)** — page content is serialized into revisions keyed generically; `PageRevisionQuerySet` filters on `base_content_type=get_default_page_content_type()` (`revisions.py:32`). Content-type-driven, not import-driven.
- **`contrib/frontend_cache/signal_handlers.py:17`** — does `apps.get_model("wagtailcore", "Page")` and `issubclass(model, Page)` at runtime, then connects `page_published`/`page_unpublished`. It depends on the page contract *entirely through the app registry and signals* — no top-level import to grep.
- **`api/v2/signal_handlers.py:13` and `admin/wagtail_hooks.py:134`** — wire behavior by iterating `get_page_models()` / registering `PageLogEntry` against `Page`. Config-time registration, invisible to a body-text search.
- **`coreutils.make_wagtail_template_fragment_key` (`coreutils.py:554`) and `get_dummy_request` (`:394`)** — utility helpers with generic names that quietly assume the page URL contract (`get_url`).
- **`contrib/settings/models.py:68` (`AbstractSetting.page_url`/`get_page_url`)** — a *settings* model that resolves arbitrary attributes to page URLs; lives far from the page subsystem and doesn't look page-related.
- **`blocks/field_block.py:967` (`PageChooserBlock`)** — reaches the model via a string `target_model` resolved through `resolve_model_string` (`:881`), i.e. by configuration, not import.
- **`locks.py:22` and `simple_translation/forms.py:53`,`:79`** — branch on `isinstance(obj, Page)` deep inside generic locking/translation code; the dependency is a runtime type check, not a query.
- **`admin/forms/models.py:74`** — builds a page FK form field generically (`to=Page`) inside the generic model-form machinery.
- **Management commands `set_url_paths.py:6` and `fixtree.py:11`** — operate directly on `url_path`/tree integrity; buried under `management/commands/` where nobody looks during a model refactor.

## Step 7 — Change + verification map (hand-off)

For each subsystem: **what to check when the contract changes.**

**Serving / routing** — `views.py:16`, `url_routing.py:1`, `pages.py:499/910/1271`, `sites.py:147/218`. Check that `route_for_request`/`route`/`serve` still resolve; `root_page.url_path` + `locale` still feed `get_site_root_paths`; re-run a live request end-to-end.

**Query layer** — `query.py:20/138/314/655/766`. If `live`/`path`/`depth`/`show_in_menus`/`content_type` move to satellites, every `live()`/`in_menu()`/`descendant_of()`/`specific()` needs its lookup path updated; verify `.specific()` still spans the new tables.

**Templating / rich text** — `wagtailcore_tags.py:18/34/52`, `rich_text/pages.py:8`. Confirm `get_url*` + `slug` still resolve after the split.

**Actions** — `actions/*` (8 classes). Confirm publishing-state and tree mutations write to the new locations; these are the write side of `live`/`first_published_at`/`expire_at`/`alias_of`.

**Permissions** — `permission_policies/pages.py:8`, `pages.py:2188`; FK `GroupPagePermission` (`pages.py:2154`). Verify path-prefix comparisons (`pages.py:2203`) and `locked_by` reads still hold; migrate the FK if the page PK changes.

**Locking** — `locks.py:11/234`. Verify `locked`/`locked_at`/`locked_by`/`go_live_at` reads.

**Admin views** — `admin/views/pages/*` (full CRUD list in Step 3). Check `.specific`, `get_children`/`get_descendants`, `live`/`has_unpublished_changes`/`latest_revision`, `content_type` at each; smoke-test create/edit/move/copy/delete/publish in the UI.

**Admin UI / panels / widgets / viewsets / reports** — `site_summary.py:20`, `action_menu.py:15`, `ui/tables/pages.py:8-109`, `ui/side_panels.py:237`, `widgets/chooser.py:221/332`, `viewsets/pages.py:58/163/578`, `panels/page_utils.py:13`, `panels/publishing_panel.py:11`, reports (`aging_pages.py:31`, `locked_pages.py:41`, `page_types_usage.py`, `workflows.py:42`), `admin/models.py:30`, `mail.py:193`, `userbar.py:254`. Verify status columns, publishing panel fields, and report queries against moved fields.

**Admin API + Public API** — `admin/api/{views,serializers,actions}`, `api/v2/views.py:437`, `filters.py:161/193/217`, `serializers.py:54/72/95/131`, `signal_handlers.py:13`. Verify tree filters and `type`/`locale`/`html_url` serialization; hit the endpoints.

**Sites** — `sites.py:90` (FK `root_page`), caching at `:239-261`. Migrate FK if PK changes; bust `get_site_root_paths` cache.

**Generic-reference layer (highest silent risk)** — `reference_index.py:119`, `revisions.py:251` (+ `:32` base-content-type filter), `workflows.py:45/99`, `pages.py:2629` (`PageLogEntry`), and the two generic relations `pages.py:367/381`. Verify `base_content_type`/`object_id` resolution still returns pages; re-run `wagtail_hooks.py:134` and `api/v2/signal_handlers.py:register_signal_handlers`.

**Contrib** — redirects (`models.py:33` FK, signal handlers `:46/66`), forms (`models.py:45` FK, `:172` FormMixin), search_promotions (`models.py:104` FK), settings (`models.py:68`), sitemaps (`sitemap_generator.py:9`), frontend_cache (`signal_handlers.py:17`, `utils.py:108/119`), routable_page (`RoutablePageMixin`), simple_translation (`forms.py:53/79`). Check each FK migration and each signal binding; frontend_cache and sitemaps are visitor-facing.

**Comments/subscriptions** — `pages.py:2669/2796/2843` (FKs `:2675/2849`). Migrate FKs.

**Users / commands** — `users/forms.py:279/309`; `management/commands/{fixtree,set_url_paths,publish_scheduled,move_pages,create_log_entries_from_revisions}.py`. Re-run `set_url_paths`/`fixtree` after any `url_path`/tree change.

**Signals (core)** — `signal_handlers.py:31/38/66/108`. Confirm page pre_delete/post_delete and reference-index update handlers still bind to `Page` and page subclasses.
